 {"id":"caam-fu2w","title":"EPIC: Live Usage Monitor & Alerts","description":"# EPIC: Live Usage Monitor

## Strategic Context

When managing multiple AI accounts, users want real-time visibility into the status of all accounts. This is different from the TUI (which focuses on profile management) - this is a MONITORING dashboard that shows live rate limit status, refresh continuously, and alerts when any account needs attention.

## Use Cases

1. **Background Monitoring**: Keep a terminal window showing all account status
2. **tmux/Screen Integration**: Show status in status bar
3. **Alert Notifications**: Desktop notifications when limits approaching
4. **Scripting Integration**: Machine-readable status for custom workflows

## User Experience

### Interactive Monitor
```
$ caam monitor

╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    LIVE USAGE MONITOR                                  ║
║                    Last updated: 12:34:56                              ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║                                                                         ║
║  CLAUDE                                                                 ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  alice@gmail.com  🟢 ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░ 42% │ resets 4h 12m │ ready  ║
║  bob@gmail.com    🟡 ██████████████░░░░░░ 71% │ resets 1h 45m │ ready  ║
║  carol@gmail.com  🔴 ████████████████████ 95% │ resets 0h 23m │ cooldown║
║                                                                         ║
║  CODEX                                                                  ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  work@company.com 🟢 ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 18% │ resets 3h 56m │ ready  ║
║                                                                         ║
║  GEMINI                                                                 ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  (no profiles configured)                                               ║
║                                                                         ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
  Refresh: 30s │ [r] refresh now │ [p] precheck │ [q] quit
```

### tmux Integration
```bash
# In .tmux.conf
set -g status-right '#(caam monitor --format brief)'

# Shows: claude:42%▲ codex:18%▲
```

### Alert Mode
```
$ caam monitor --alert --threshold 80

[2024-01-10 12:34:56] ⚠️  claude/carol@gmail.com at 85%
[2024-01-10 12:35:12] 🔴 claude/carol@gmail.com at 90%
[2024-01-10 12:35:45] 🔴 claude/carol@gmail.com at 95% - SWITCH RECOMMENDED
```

## Implementation

### Monitor State
```go
// internal/monitor/monitor.go

type Monitor struct {
    interval   time.Duration
    providers  []string
    
    fetcher    *usage.MultiProfileFetcher
    vault      *authfile.Vault
    healthStore *health.Storage
    db         *db.DB
    
    state      *MonitorState
    mu         sync.RWMutex
}

type MonitorState struct {
    Profiles  map[string]*ProfileState  // "provider/name" -> state
    UpdatedAt time.Time
    Errors    []string
}

type ProfileState struct {
    Provider     string
    ProfileName  string
    Usage        *usage.UsageInfo
    Health       health.HealthStatus
    PoolStatus   authpool.PoolStatus
    InCooldown   bool
    CooldownUntil *time.Time
    Alert        *Alert
}

type Alert struct {
    Type    AlertType
    Message string
    Since   time.Time
}
```

### Output Renderers
```go
// internal/monitor/render.go

type Renderer interface {
    Render(state *MonitorState) string
}

type TableRenderer struct {}    // Rich ASCII table
type BriefRenderer struct {}    // One-line summary
type JSONRenderer struct {}     // Machine-readable
type AlertRenderer struct {}    // Alerts only
```

### Alert System
```go
// internal/monitor/alerts.go

type AlertManager struct {
    threshold   float64
    notifier    Notifier
    history     []*Alert
}

type Notifier interface {
    Notify(alert *Alert) error
}

type DesktopNotifier struct {}  // libnotify / osascript
type TerminalNotifier struct {} // Print to stderr
type WebhookNotifier struct {}  // POST to URL
```

### Background Polling
```go
func (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return nil
        case <-ticker.C:
            m.refresh(ctx)
        }
    }
}

func (m *Monitor) refresh(ctx context.Context) {
    // 1. List all profiles from vault
    // 2. Fetch usage for each (concurrent)
    // 3. Check health status
    // 4. Check cooldown status
    // 5. Generate alerts
    // 6. Update state
}
```

## Commands

### caam monitor
```
Usage:
  caam monitor [flags]

Flags:
  -i, --interval duration   Refresh interval (default 30s)
  -p, --provider strings    Providers to monitor (default: all)
  -f, --format string       Output format: table, brief, json, alerts
  -a, --alert               Enable alert mode (log alerts to stderr)
  -t, --threshold float     Alert threshold (default 0.8)
  -n, --notify              Enable desktop notifications
  -w, --webhook string      Webhook URL for alerts
  -1, --once                Fetch once and exit (no live monitoring)
```

### Examples
```bash
# Interactive monitor
caam monitor

# tmux status bar integration
caam monitor --format brief --once

# Alert mode with desktop notifications
caam monitor --alert --notify --threshold 80

# JSON output for scripting
caam monitor --format json --once | jq '.profiles[] | select(.usage.primary_percent > 80)'

# Monitor specific provider
caam monitor --provider claude
```

## Integration Points

### TUI Integration
Add monitor panel to existing TUI (internal/tui/):
- Real-time usage bars for each profile
- Alert indicators
- Quick switch actions

### Daemon Integration  
Monitor can run as part of daemon for:
- Background alert generation
- Desktop notifications
- Webhook triggers

## Deliverables
1. Monitor struct and state management
2. Background polling loop
3. Table renderer (rich ASCII)
4. Brief renderer (one-line)
5. JSON renderer
6. Alert system with thresholds
7. Desktop notification integration
8. Webhook notification
9. caam monitor command
10. TUI integration (optional)

## Dependencies
- internal/usage/ (for fetching rate limits)
- internal/health/ (for health status)
- internal/db/ (for cooldown status)
- EPIC: Background Auth Pool Daemon (for pool status)

## Enables
- Real-time visibility into account status
- Proactive notifications before limits hit
- tmux/screen integration for always-visible status
- Scripting and automation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:16:21.51807078-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T00:16:21.51807078-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-fu2w","depends_on_id":"caam-3qdv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:20:48.810497527-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-fwl","title":"SQLite database setup and migrations","description":"## Purpose
Set up SQLite database infrastructure for usage analytics and activity tracking.

## Background
SQLite was chosen over BoltDB (used by codex-pool) for its query flexibility.
We need a migration system to handle schema evolution as features are added.

## Technical Requirements

### Database Location
```
~/.caam/data/caam.db
```

### Migration System
```go
// internal/db/migrations.go
type Migration struct {
    Version int
    Name    string
    Up      string
    Down    string
}

var migrations = []Migration{
    {
        Version: 1,
        Name:    "initial_schema",
        Up: `
            CREATE TABLE schema_version (
                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                applied_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            );
            
            CREATE TABLE activity_log (
                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
                event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
                provider TEXT NOT NULL,
                profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                details TEXT,
                duration_seconds INTEGER
            );
            
            CREATE TABLE profile_stats (
                provider TEXT NOT NULL,
                profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                total_activations INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                total_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                total_active_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                last_activated DATETIME,
                last_error DATETIME,
                PRIMARY KEY (provider, profile_name)
            );
            
            CREATE INDEX idx_activity_timestamp ON activity_log(timestamp);
            CREATE INDEX idx_activity_provider ON activity_log(provider, profile_name);
        `,
    },
}

func RunMigrations(db *sql.DB) error {
    // Apply pending migrations
}
```

### Database Manager
```go
// internal/db/db.go
type DB struct {
    conn *sql.DB
}

func Open() (*DB, error) {
    // Ensure directory exists
    // Open connection with WAL mode
    // Run migrations
}

func (d *DB) Close() error
```

## Dependencies
- Uses modernc.org/sqlite (CGO-free driver)
- Directory utilities from existing caam code

## Files to Create
- internal/db/db.go
- internal/db/migrations.go
- internal/db/db_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Database created automatically on first use
- [ ] Migrations run idempotently
- [ ] WAL mode enabled for concurrent reads
- [ ] Tests verify migration system works
- [ ] Graceful handling of corrupt database","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:11.418686244-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T19:57:08.742627263-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:57:08.742627263-05:00","close_reason":"Added SQLite DB infrastructure with migrations, WAL mode, and corruption recovery.","comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"caam-fwl","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting SQLite DB + migrations: add internal/db with Open (WAL mode) + migration runner + tests (including corrupt DB handling). Will use modernc.org/sqlite per spec.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:52:57Z"},{"id":18,"issue_id":"caam-fwl","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented internal/db with modernc.org/sqlite: Open/OpenAt creates ~/.caam/data/caam.db (or CAAM_HOME), enables WAL + foreign_keys, runs idempotent migrations, and auto-renames corrupt DB files before recreating. Added migration + Open tests.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:56:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-fwz","title":"OAuth refresh handler for Claude Code","description":"# OAuth Refresh Handler for Claude Code

## Purpose
Implement OAuth token refresh for Claude Code to automatically renew tokens before expiry.

## Research Required
**IMPORTANT**: Claude Code OAuth implementation needs reverse engineering.

### Questions to Answer
1. Where is the oauth.json file located?
2. What is the token endpoint URL?
3. What is the client_id?
4. Is it JSON body or form-encoded?
5. What scopes are required?

### Likely Locations to Check
- `~/.claude/oauth.json`
- `~/.claude.ai/oauth.json`  
- `~/.config/claude/oauth.json`
- Check Claude Code source/docs if available

## Technical Requirements (Template)

### Refresh Function
```go
func RefreshClaudeToken(ctx context.Context, refreshToken string) (*TokenResponse, error) {
    // Build request
    body := map[string]string{
        "client_id":     claudeClientID,
        "grant_type":    "refresh_token",
        "refresh_token": refreshToken,
        // "scope":      "...",  // if needed
    }
    
    req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", claudeTokenURL, ...)
    req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")  // or form-encoded
    
    // Execute
    resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("refresh request failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer resp.Body.Close()
    
    // Parse response
    if resp.StatusCode != 200 {
        // Handle error
    }
    
    var tokenResp TokenResponse
    json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&tokenResp)
    
    return &tokenResp, nil
}
```

### TokenResponse Structure
```go
type TokenResponse struct {
    AccessToken  string `json:"access_token"`
    RefreshToken string `json:"refresh_token,omitempty"` // May rotate
    ExpiresIn    int    `json:"expires_in"`              // Seconds
    ExpiresAt    string `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`    // ISO8601
    TokenType    string `json:"token_type"`
}
```

### File Update
```go
func UpdateClaudeAuth(path string, resp *TokenResponse) error {
    // Read existing file
    existing, _ := os.ReadFile(path)
    var auth map[string]any
    json.Unmarshal(existing, &auth)
    
    // Update fields
    auth["access_token"] = resp.AccessToken
    if resp.RefreshToken != "" {
        auth["refresh_token"] = resp.RefreshToken
    }
    auth["expires_at"] = resp.ExpiresAt
    // ... or calculate from expires_in
    
    // Atomic write
    return atomicWriteJSON(path, auth)
}
```

## Safety Requirements
1. **Never lose refresh token** - if refresh fails, keep original
2. **Atomic writes** - use temp file + rename
3. **Backup original** - keep .bak file before overwrite
4. **Verify new token** - optionally test before committing

## Error Handling
- Invalid refresh token → mark profile as "needs login"
- Network error → retry with backoff
- Rate limited → respect Retry-After header
- Unknown error → log and keep original token

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Research: Document Claude Code OAuth endpoints
- [ ] RefreshClaudeToken function implemented
- [ ] UpdateClaudeAuth with atomic writes
- [ ] Unit tests with mock HTTP server
- [ ] Integration test with real credentials (optional, manual)
- [ ] Error handling for all failure modes

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/refresh/claude.go` (new)
- `internal/refresh/claude_test.go` (new)

## Dependencies
- Health metadata storage (for updating expiry)
- Token expiry parsing (for reading current state)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:20:23.871008574-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:35:02.534599402-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:35:02.534599402-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented OAuth refresh logic for Claude Code. Includes RefreshClaudeToken and UpdateClaudeAuth with atomic writes. Endpoint and Client ID are placeholders needing verification.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-fwz","depends_on_id":"caam-thb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:54.952222175-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"caam-fwz","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
Follow-up from caam-dt4: added token endpoint allowlist guard (prevents refresh tokens being posted to non-provider hosts except loopback for tests) and updated CLI to treat refresh unsupported as skipped. Claude endpoint/client_id still best-guess placeholders; needs verification if we want guaranteed Claude refresh support.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:32:11Z"}]}
-{"id":"caam-fyzn","title":"EPIC: Background Auth Pool Daemon","description":"# EPIC: Background Auth Pool Daemon

## Strategic Context

For Smart Session Handoff to work instantly, backup accounts must have FRESH, VALID tokens ready to use. If we wait until the handoff moment to check token validity, we add latency and risk failure.

The solution: A background daemon that keeps ALL accounts "warm" - monitoring token expiry and proactively refreshing before they expire.

## Background

### Current State
caam has a daemon (internal/daemon/) but it's currently used for:
- Background profile watching
- Hot reload of TUI

It does NOT currently:
- Monitor token expiry across all profiles
- Proactively refresh tokens
- Maintain a "pool" of ready-to-use accounts

### CodexBar's Approach
CodexBar (specifically for Augment provider) has "session keepalive" - background polling to maintain cookies. This is similar in spirit but simpler than what we need.

### Token Refresh in caam
caam already has token refresh logic (internal/refresh/):
- claude.go: Refresh Claude OAuth tokens
- codex.go: Refresh Codex tokens
- gemini.go: Refresh Gemini tokens
- refresh.go: Common refresh interface

This works but is REACTIVE (refresh when expired/needed), not PROACTIVE (refresh before needed).

## Technical Approach

### Auth Pool Manager
```go
// internal/authpool/pool.go

type PooledProfile struct {
    Provider     string
    ProfileName  string
    TokenExpiry  time.Time
    LastRefresh  time.Time
    LastCheck    time.Time
    Status       PoolStatus  // Ready, Refreshing, Expired, Error
    ErrorCount   int
    ErrorMessage string
}

type PoolStatus int
const (
    PoolStatusUnknown PoolStatus = iota
    PoolStatusReady      // Token valid, ready to use
    PoolStatusRefreshing // Currently refreshing
    PoolStatusExpired    // Token expired, needs refresh
    PoolStatusError      // Refresh failed
)

type AuthPool struct {
    profiles map[string]*PooledProfile  // "provider/name" -> profile
    mu       sync.RWMutex
    
    refreshThreshold time.Duration  // Refresh when TTL < this (default: 15min)
    checkInterval    time.Duration  // How often to check (default: 5min)
    maxRetries       int            // Max refresh retries before marking Error
    
    vault    *authfile.Vault
    refresher map[string]refresh.Refresher  // provider -> refresher
}
```

### Background Monitor Loop
```go
func (p *AuthPool) MonitorLoop(ctx context.Context) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(p.checkInterval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        case <-ticker.C:
            p.checkAllProfiles()
        }
    }
}

func (p *AuthPool) checkAllProfiles() {
    p.mu.RLock()
    profiles := make([]*PooledProfile, 0, len(p.profiles))
    for _, prof := range p.profiles {
        profiles = append(profiles, prof)
    }
    p.mu.RUnlock()
    
    for _, prof := range profiles {
        if prof.Status == PoolStatusRefreshing {
            continue  // Already being refreshed
        }
        
        ttl := time.Until(prof.TokenExpiry)
        if ttl < p.refreshThreshold {
            go p.refreshProfile(prof)
        }
    }
}
```

### Daemon Integration
Modify internal/daemon/daemon.go to include AuthPool:

```go
type Daemon struct {
    // Existing
    watcher  *watcher.Watcher
    
    // NEW
    authPool *authpool.AuthPool
}

func (d *Daemon) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
    // Start existing watchers
    go d.watcher.Watch(ctx)
    
    // NEW: Start auth pool monitor
    go d.authPool.MonitorLoop(ctx)
}
```

### Pool State Persistence
Save pool state to disk so daemon can resume after restart:
- Location: ~/.local/share/caam/auth_pool.json
- Contains: Last known state of each profile
- Updated: On each successful refresh

## Deliverables
1. AuthPool struct and manager
2. Background monitor loop with configurable intervals
3. Proactive token refresh integration
4. Pool state persistence
5. Daemon integration
6. CLI commands: caam pool status, caam pool refresh
7. Health endpoint for pool status

## Success Criteria
- All profiles refreshed before expiry (>99% success rate)
- No profile ever expires unexpectedly
- Sub-second handoff latency (tokens already fresh)
- Graceful handling of refresh failures
- Minimal resource usage (background daemon should be lightweight)

## Configuration
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
auth_pool:
  enabled: true
  check_interval: 5m
  refresh_threshold: 15m  # Refresh when TTL < this
  max_retries: 3
  retry_backoff: 30s
```

## Dependencies
- internal/refresh/ (existing token refresh logic)
- internal/daemon/ (existing daemon infrastructure)

## Enables
- EPIC: Smart Session Handoff (instant handoff with pre-refreshed tokens)
- Reliable multi-account rotation","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:13:59.339394015-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T00:13:59.339394015-05:00"}

2. Background monitor loop with configurable intervals
3. Proactive token refresh integration
4. Pool state persistence
5. Daemon integration
6. CLI commands: caam pool status, caam pool refresh
7. Health endpoint for pool status

## Success Criteria
- All profiles refreshed before expiry (>99% success rate)
- No profile ever expires unexpectedly
- Sub-second handoff latency (tokens already fresh)
- Graceful handling of refresh failures
- Minimal resource usage (background daemon should be lightweight)

  enabled: true

- internal/refresh/ (existing token refresh logic)
- internal/daemon/ (existing daemon infrastructure)

 {"id":"caam-g2yz.1","title":"Profile Description Field","description":"## Overview
Add a `Description` field to the Profile struct for free-form notes about what each profile is used for.

## Problem Statement
Users with multiple profiles struggle to remember what each profile is for. The current `AccountLabel` field is semantic for email/account identification, not purpose descriptions. Users need a place for notes like:
- "Client X project"
- "Free tier for testing"
- "Team shared account - billing goes to ops@company.com"
- "Personal pro account - don't use for client work"

## Design Decisions

### Single Field vs Multiple Fields
**Decision:** Single `Description` string field
**Rationale:** Keeping it simple. No need for separate "short" and "long" descriptions. Users can write whatever they want. If they need structure, they can use their own conventions.

### Field Properties
- **Type:** `Description string`
- **JSON tag:** `json:"description,omitempty"`
- **omitempty:** Yes - no migration needed for existing profiles
- **Max length:** Recommended 200 chars, not enforced (user responsibility)
- **Validation:** None - free-form text, trust the user

### Display Contexts
Description appears in:
1. `caam ls` - truncated to ~40 chars if long, with "..." indicator
2. `caam status` - shown when relevant profile is active
3. `caam profile show <tool> <profile>` - full description
4. TUI profile list - truncated to fit column
5. TUI profile detail panel - full description

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Profile Struct Enhancement (internal/profile/profile.go)
```go
type Profile struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    Description string `json:"description,omitempty"` // Free-form notes
}
```

### 2. CLI: Add Flag to `caam add` and `caam profile add`
- Add `--description/-d` flag
- Example: `caam add claude --name work --description "Client consulting projects"`

### 3. CLI: Create `caam profile describe` Command
```
caam profile describe <tool> <profile> [description]
```
- If description provided: set it
- If no description: show current description (or prompt interactively?)
- Add `--clear` flag to remove description

### 4. Update Display Commands
- `caam ls`: Add description column (truncated)
- `caam profile show`: Include description in output
- `caam status`: Show description of active profile

### 5. TUI Integration
- Profile list: Show description in secondary text
- Profile detail: Display full description
- Add description editing capability (future enhancement, not MVP)

## Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for Profile serialization with description
- Integration test for describe command
- Test truncation logic for display
- Test backward compatibility (profiles without description)

## Backward Compatibility
Fully backward compatible - existing profiles without description field work unchanged due to omitempty tag.

## Files to Modify
- internal/profile/profile.go - Add field
- cmd/caam/cmd/add.go - Add flag
- cmd/caam/cmd/profile.go - Create describe subcommand, update show
- cmd/caam/cmd/list.go - Update output formatting
- cmd/caam/cmd/status.go - Show description
- internal/tui/profiles.go - Display in list and detail

## Success Criteria

 {"id":"caam-g2yz.2","title":"Profile Tags for Categorization","description":"## Overview
Add a tagging system for categorizing profiles into groups.

## ⚠️ Priority Note: P3 (Backlog)
This feature adds significant complexity while favorites already provide profile organization. Consider this a "nice to have" after core features are solid.

**Alternative:** Could the favorites system be extended instead? E.g., "favorites groups" or "favorite sets"?

## Problem Statement
As profile counts grow, users need ways to categorize and filter profiles beyond simple listing. Tags enable organizational views by project, team, environment, client, etc.

## Key Distinction from Favorites
- **Favorites:** Ordered priority list (1st favorite, 2nd favorite, etc.)
- **Tags:** Unordered categories (work, personal, project-x, testing)

However, the overlap is significant. Users with 5-10 profiles may not need both systems.

## Design Decisions

### Tag Format
**Decision:** Simple string tags (not key:value pairs)
**Constraints:**
- Lowercase, alphanumeric + hyphens only
- Max 32 characters per tag
- Max 10 tags per profile

### Storage
```go
type Profile struct {
    Tags []string \`json:"tags,omitempty"\`
}
```

## Implementation Tasks
1. Add Tags field to Profile struct
2. Create `caam tag add/remove/list` commands (or `caam profile tag ...`)
3. Add `--tag` filter to `caam ls`
4. TUI integration

## Recommendation
Defer this until users explicitly request it.
